findings.description: The PDF uses a clear two-section layout with 'ESTIMATED RESOURCES' (Total, Federal, State, County) and 'ESTIMATED PERCENT' (Federal, State, County) as distinct column groupings with a visual separator. The HTML renders all columns in a flat table without the grouped header structure.
findings.evidence: PDF has a two-tier header with 'ESTIMATED RESOURCES' spanning Total/Federal/State/County and 'ESTIMATED PERCENT' spanning Federal/State/County. HTML shows individual column headers without the grouping row.

findings.description: The HTML table splits the 'Total Cost For' summary row into the same table structure as line items, losing the visual distinction of the bold summary row that spans across in the PDF.
findings.evidence: PDF shows 'Total Cost For' as a distinct summary row with bold formatting. HTML preserves the text but the visual hierarchy is less distinct.

findings.description: Same grouped header issue on page 2 - the two-tier column grouping (ESTIMATED RESOURCES / ESTIMATED PERCENT) is flattened in the HTML.
findings.evidence: PDF has grouped column headers; HTML shows flat column headers.

findings.description: Same grouped header issue on page 3.
findings.evidence: PDF has grouped column headers; HTML shows flat column headers.

findings.description: Section headings like 'A. Division of Social Services', 'B. Division of Aging and Adult Services', etc. appear bold and visually distinct in the PDF but have somewhat reduced visual distinction in the HTML.
findings.evidence: PDF shows clear bold section headers with horizontal rules/separators. HTML preserves bold but section separation is less pronounced.

findings.description: The Grand Total row at the bottom of page 3 is rendered in the HTML but the visual emphasis (bold, larger text, double-line border) is somewhat reduced.
findings.evidence: PDF shows 'Grand Total All Programs and Administration' with strong visual emphasis. HTML shows it as a bold row but with less visual distinction.

findings.description: The HTML contains nested <main> elements - a <main> in the body and another <main> inside it. The document title 'Jackson County' is used as h1, which is acceptable, but there are no h2/h3 headings used for section titles like '1. Public Assistance Programs', '2. Public Assistance Administration', '3. Services Programs' - these are instead rendered as bold text within table cells rather than as proper headings.
findings.evidence: PDF has clear section headings: '1. Public Assistance Programs', '2. Public Assistance Administration', '3. Services Programs', and sub-headings like 'A. Division of Social Services', 'B. Division of Aging and Adult Services', etc. HTML places all of these inside table cells with <strong> tags rather than as heading elements.

findings.description: The footer section of the HTML contains duplicated page header/footer information from all three pages. The repeating header blocks (G.S. 108A-88 citation, county name, etc.) and 'Note that the totals may be off due to rounding' appear multiple times in the footer area, duplicating content that is already present in the document header.
findings.evidence: The HTML footer contains three repetitions of the page header block with 'Page 2 of 3', 'Page 3 of 3' and three repetitions of 'Note that the totals may be off due to rounding.' These are page-level artifacts from the PDF that are unnecessarily repeated.

findings.description: The initial 'NC CORELS' section and its 'Total Cost For' line appear before '1. Public Assistance Programs' in the PDF as a standalone section under 'A. Division of Social Services'. While present in the HTML, this section lacks context - in the PDF it appears to be a separate preliminary section, but in the HTML it's merged into the same table without clear delineation.
findings.evidence: PDF page 1 shows NC CORELS with Total Cost For as a distinct section before '1. Public Assistance Programs'. HTML includes these rows but within the same continuous table.

findings.description: All numeric values, percentages, and program names in the table match the source PDF accurately across all 58 line items, subtotals, and the grand total. No data integrity issues found.
findings.evidence: Verified key values: Grand Total 12,851,566 / Federal 10,470,416 / State 553,587 / County 1,827,564 / 81.47% / 4.31% / 14.22% - all match PDF.
